title: UnifiedCommsEntry
description: One normalized communication event across channels (quo/openphone SMS+call, imessage, email/gmail, twilio). The intersection that survives every channel, plus channel-discriminated optional fields. This is the FROZEN SHAPE of the comms.recentLog combo (ALCHEMICAL_PROMOTION.md §2b) — provider responses are reshaped TO this by codemode. Modeled on the canonical contextual.messages + contextual.party_identifiers relation that imessage_unified_with_quo and quo_recent_messages_local already read. @canon: chittycanon://gov/governance#core-types

channel.description: Communication channel. Discriminator that selects the late-bound provider. Maps from the provider 'source' tag (quo rows carry source='openphone').

provider.description: Concrete provider that produced the row, when distinct from channel. Lets quo and twilio (both channel-family SMS) stay distinguishable while interchangeable. Real value observed: 'openphone' on quo rows.

providerMessageId.description: Stable id of this message in the PROVIDER namespace — quo external_id (AC...), gmail message id, twilio MessageSid (SM...), imessage guid. NOT the internal surrogate. Used for dedup across channels and for get-by-id round-trips.

internalId.description: Internal surrogate id from the local store (quo message_id '16884'). Present only when the row came from a ChittyOS local cache; opaque, do not join cross-service on it.

threadRef.description: Provider thread/conversation reference. quo external_thread_id (AC...) or conversation id (CN...), gmail threadId, imessage chat_identifier.

direction.description: Relative to the ChittyOS account/owner. Observed verbatim on quo rows.

occurredAt.description: Event time, RFC3339 / ISO-8601 UTC. Reshaped from quo sent_at, gmail date. The single sort key for the time-ordered merge.

participants.description: Normalized P-typed actors on this event. Each communicating endpoint is a Person (P) per chittycanon://gov/governance#core-types — actors with agency, never Thing(T). Built from quo parties[] {role,identifier} and gmail sender/toRecipients/ccRecipients. codemode collapses the owner's identity-alias bundle (the ~30 self-identifiers observed in quo parties[]) into a single self=true counterparty.

body.description: Full message text when available (quo body_text, imessage text, email plaintext_body). Absent for metadata-only fetches (gmail THREAD_VIEW_METADATA_ONLY).

snippet.description: Short preview. Always derivable: gmail returns snippet directly; for quo/imessage codemode truncates body. The body/snippet union — at least one of body|snippet is always present.

subject.description: Email subject line. Email-only; null/absent for SMS/iMessage which have no subject.

transcriptRef.description: Reference to a voice-call transcript (quo_get_call_transcription / call_summary). Present only for channel=voice or quo call events; lets a recent-comms log fuse calls alongside texts without inlining the transcript.

labels.description: Provider-side labels/tags (gmail labelIds[]). Lossy-optional: carried through when present, never required.

source.description: Raw provider 'source' tag exactly as stored (e.g. 'openphone'), retained for provenance and drift auditing. Distinct from the normalized channel/provider above.

_meta.description: Non-schema provenance carried per ALCHEMICAL_PROMOTION.md §5 — codemode reshape confidence, binding used, archetype. Kept out of the typed surface so a 4th channel adds nothing here.

CommParty.description: A Person (P) endpoint on a comm event. @canon: chittycanon://gov/governance#core-types — communicating endpoints are actors with agency = Person, Synthetic or Natural; never Thing.

role.description: Reshaped from quo parties[].role + gmail header position.

identifier.description: Raw endpoint identifier as the provider stored it: E.164 phone (+13122186717) or email address. The natural cross-channel join key (one Person reachable on both).

identifierKind.description: Derived by codemode from the identifier shape.

self.description: True when this identifier belongs to the ChittyOS account owner (collapses the observed ~30 owner-alias identifiers). Lets consumers pick the counterparty without re-deriving 'me'.

chittyId.description: Resolved ChittyID of the Person, T=P. Pattern verified against real production IDs (e.g. 03-1-USA-5537-P-2602-0-38): VV-G-LLL-SSSS-P-YYMM-C-XX where the year-month is 2-4 chars and the final check segment is 1-2 chars. OPTIONAL and late-resolved — the contract does not require identity resolution to succeed; an unresolved counterparty still produces a valid entry keyed by raw identifier.

displayName.description: Human label when the provider supplies one (quo contact name, email display name).
